zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Elizabeth Grandison於1336年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾阿培頓時，她的父親Peter de Grandison 2nd Baron Grandison39歲，母親Blanche de Mortimer23歲。她於大約1354年與Baldwin De Brugge結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1406年4月4日在英國英格蘭賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Grandison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): said to be a habitational name from Granson on Lake Neuchâtel (Switzerland).

History: The first known bearer of this surname is Rigaldus de Grancione (flourished 1040). The name was taken to Britain by Otes de Grandison (died 1328) and his brother. They were among a group of Savoyards who settled in England when Henry III married a granddaughter of the Count of Savoy.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Elizabeth Grandison was born in 1336, in Ashperton, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Peter de Grandison 2nd Baron Grandison, was 39 and her mother, Blanche de Mortimer, was 23. She married Baldwin De Brugge about 1354. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 4 April 1406, in Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

Elle a épousé Baldwin De Brugge environ 1354. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 4 avril 1406, à Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Grandisonが1336年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアアシュペルトンで生まれた時，父Peter de Grandison 2nd Baron Grandisonは39歳，母Blanche de Mortimerは23歳であった。約1354年にBaldwin De Bruggeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1406年4月4日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブリッジ ソラーズで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Grandison(이)가 1336년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 애쉬퍼턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Peter de Grandison 2nd Baron Grandison(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Blanche de Mortimer(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1354년 Baldwin De Brugge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1406년 4월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 브리지 솔레르스에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.
